rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with proper labeling and expression formatting.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features from the AADL model.
Generated AGREE contracts must use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and logical operators.
Equation declarations should define helper variables with explicit types and boolean expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`
Constants and equations use `const name : type = value;` and `eq name : type = expression;`
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponents or AADL properties directly in type-level contracts.
Do not generate expressions with unresolved identifiers or mismatched types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage
